source_jobs.title: Data Scientist – Lilongwe
source_jobs.description:
BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - +3 years of experience in machine learning, preferably in computer vision

 - Respectable track record in competitive data modeling, and/or notable research achievements.

 - Well-versed with the latest trends with top-performing tools and the ML research community.

 - Strong fluency in both Python and in the main AI-related tools of the trade

 - Strong skills in logical reasoning and critical thinking, and the confidence to both express it and also receive constructive feedback through daily code review

 - Non-superficial intuitions about AI, beyond import blackbox

 - Highly preferred full-time employment of at least 40 hours per week. Promising students with exceptional skills should contribute at least 24 hrs/week.

 - Good fluency in English speaking and writing (typing > 35 wpm).

 - Critical thinking skills, expressiveness, and team-oriented attitude.

 - And you have to care about the work that you do!


NICE TO HAVES

 - Working knowledge of statistics and basic probability.

 - Experience working with enterprise data warehouse software and/or data processing pipelines.

 - Experience working with datasets that cannot fit on a desktop, and systems engineering techniques for overcoming these constraints.

 - Evidence of leadership and team-building skills.

source_jobs.title: Information Specialist – Warsaw
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils, and surveillance of HIV and malaria at national-scale in multiple African countries. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Gates Foundation, the Polish Ministry of Agriculture, EU funding, the Global Fund, and many more.



We are looking for an Information Specialist to join our team in Warsaw, Poland or Lilongwe, Malawi, to create effective and practical strategies for organizing vast troves of information in both the health and agricultural sciences.




BACKGROUND

The Global South and the African continent is peppered with vast mountain ranges of disorganized paper-based data. These handwritten forms are not going away anytime soon due to systemic weaknesses in electrical, networking, and computer infrastructure. And yet, every sector of government, including the Ministries of Health, Agriculture, and Education, desperately need data to optimize their programs under increasingly strained budgets.

At QED.ai http://QED.ai we have been designing smart forms to electronically capture handwritten data at national scale. Now, we are looking for Library Science and Information Architecture students and graduates who welcome the challenge of organizing this growing body of information.

You’ll contribute to building well-structured, intuitive tools for capturing data efficiently, and standardizing unique identifiers for specimens, patients, cards, pages, books, diseases, diagnoses, and treatments. This data must be made searchable, sortable, and linkable to quickly identify trends and patterns. You’ll work closely with end users to understand their needs and help them extract relevant information.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Assist with the co-design of data capture and data management solutions for our clients. This design should incorporate crafting of questionnaires, layout of data elements, visual aesthetics, specimen identifiers, the speed of healthcare workers, and error correction codes for identifiers.

 - Write logical queries to extract and transform data. Design clear, user-centered dashboards that support information discovery and decision-making, using principles from information architecture, user experience (UX), and data storytelling.

 - Engage with Ministries of Health and Agriculture in Africa, as well as international aid organizations such as the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), UNICEF, and donors, to understand current data processing workflows and pains, and gather their requirements for data capture and data analysis .

 - Applying visual design principles to produce informative and aesthetic training materials.

 - Writing clear and concise user instructions and documentation.
   
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - Completed or pursuing formal academic studies in library science, information architecture, or related fields.

 - Working proficiency (≥C1) in speaking, reading, and typing English and Polish (≥45 words per minute). Official certificate is not necessary.

 - Must be open to international travel to partner institutions or project sites. Culturally adaptive and adventurous!

 - Logical reasoning and ability to express ideas clearly, both orally and in writing.

 - At ease using computers and digital systems.

 - Willingness and interest in working with people from other cultures.

 - Emotional resilience and at least mediocre social intelligence.

 - … and you have to care about the work that you do!



Below are additional skills that are a bonus, but are not required:

 - some experience or exposure to the natural sciences, such as medicine, chemistry, biology, agronomy, or environmental science

 - foreign language skills, especially in French, Portuguese, Swahili, Chichewa, or Chinese

 - domain knowledge a

source_jobs.title: ScanForm Field Coordinator – Nigeria
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai http://QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and artificial intelligence that empowers the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including epidemiological surveillance of HIV and malaria, and in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils using spectroscopy and computer vision. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Global Fund, CDC, USAID, and the Gates Foundation.

We are looking for a ScanForm Field Coordinator.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Coordinate the deployment of ScanForm, working with in-country MOH and IP teams to ensure smooth roll-out, including testing, multiple levels of training, distribution, and monitoring and evaluation.

 - Use diplomacy to establish good relationships with all parties and diffuse issues as they arise.

 - Compose field reports and summaries.

 - Identify problems, diagnose causes, and resolve or delegate tickets to appropriate technical staff.

 - Liaise with government to support data use and monitoring and evaluation to drive public policy.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Residency in Nigeria.

 - Experience with field work and coordination of field teams.

 - Fluency in multiple languages, including both English and local languages in the country of residence.

 - Emotional intelligence and strong face-to-face communications skills.

 - Experience and comfort with making public presentations.

 - High proficiency in speaking, reading, and composing clear and correct English.

 - English typing speed of at least 40 words per minute.

 - Strong logical reasoning and attention to detail.

 - Experience with composing field reports and summaries.

 - Experience working with Excel spreadsheets.

 - Ability to listen carefully to our clients and communicate their desires accurately back to the team.

 - Ability to juggle substantial quantities of facts and figures.

 - Enthusiasm for technology and optimization.

 - Highly preferred full-time employment of at least 40 hours per week. Promising students with exceptional skills should contribute at least 24 hrs/week.

 - Critical thinking skills, expressiveness, and team-oriented attitude.

 - And you have to care about the work that you do!


NICE TO HAVES

 - Domain expertise or experience working in public health on the ground.

 - Some experience with monitoring and evaluation, or basic programming, or basic statistics.

 - Experience with video screencasting.

source_jobs.title: Math Instructor - Primary Years
source_jobs.description:
Early Years Math Instructor



The QED Africa Foundation (https://qed.ai) is focused on improving public health, food security, and education. We bring the hacker spirit to global challenges faced in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. QED is partnered with S.A.M. https://seriouslyaddictivemaths.com.sg/ (Seriously Addictive Maths), a globally recognized Singaporean math enrichment program focused on building excellent numeracy, logical thinking, and problem-solving skills in children.



We are hiring for an Early Years Math Instructor (Ages 4–6), focused on introducing young learners to numbers, patterns, and basic problem-solving in a fun, structured, and engaging way.



 - Key Responsibilities
   
   - Teach foundational math concepts, like counting, multiplication, using hands-on and guided approaches
   
   - Engage students through interactive, age-appropriate instruction
   
   - Build students’ confidence and positive attitude toward mathematics
   
   - Provide close individual attention and encouragement
   
   - Communicate regularly with parents on student progress

 - Ideal Candidate Profile
   
   - Enjoys working with young children (nursery and early primary)
   
   - Energetic, patient, and highly engaging
   
   - Strong communication skills with both children and parents
   
   - Background in Early Childhood Education is an advantage
   
   - Comfortable using structured teaching methods

 - General Requirements
   
   - Strong in arithmetic, logical reasoning, and number sense
   
   - 2+ years of experience with working with young children
   
   - Native English proficiency (≥C2) in speaking, reading, and typing
   
   - Comfortable with teaching in both English and Chichewa
   
   - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
   
   - Energetic attitude and spirit.
   
   - Professionalism, reliability, and willingness to learn
   
   - Familiarity with basic desktop publishing software, such as Google Docs/Slides/Sheets and Microsoft Word/Excel/Powerpoint, WhatsApp
   
   - +3 years prior work experience preferred

 - What We Offer
   
   - Comprehensive and certified training in the S.A.M methodology
   
   - Opportunity to be part of a pioneering math education initiative in Malawi
   
   - Supportive and professional work environment
   
   - Part-time engagement can be arranged
   
   - Competitive compensation based on experience



Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Apply at: https://qed.ai/jobs

source_jobs.title: ScanForm Support Technician for HIV programs – Côte d'Ivoire
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai http://QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and artificial intelligence that empowers the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including epidemiological surveillance of HIV and malaria, and in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils using spectroscopy and computer vision. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Global Fund, CDC, and the Gates Foundation.

We are looking for a ScanForm Support Technician for HIV programs in Côte d’Ivoire.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Provision of technical support and assistance to government and implementing partners with data quality monitoring and evaluation. Identify problems, diagnose causes, and resolve or delegate tickets to appropriate staff.

 - Remote technical assistance guided by data, answering phone calls reactively and also making phone calls proactively to improve data quality and correct use of registers and workflows.

 - In-person training, mentorship, and supportive supervision during regular site supervision, in coordination with project partners.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Residency in Côte d'Ivoire.

 - Strong experience working within the health system, including exposure to facility- or community-based health programs.

 - Understanding of health information systems (HIS) and routine health data collection processes.

 - Experience providing mentorship, on-the-job support, or technical assistance to health workers or data staff.

 - Native or near-native fluency in French.

 - Knowledge of local languages used in health facilities is a plus.

 - Knows HIV guidelines and the HIV testing algorithm by heart.

 - Patience.

 - Emotional intelligence and strong face-to-face communication skills.

 - Strong logical reasoning and attention to detail.

 - Ability to compose field reports and summaries.

 - Ability to listen carefully to clients and partners and communicate their needs accurately back to the technical team.

 - Experience and comfort with making public presentations.

 - High proficiency in spoken and written English, with the ability to compose clear and accurate written communication.

 - English typing speed of at least 40 words per minute.

 - Highly preferred full-time employment of at least 40 hours per week. Promising students with exceptional skills should contribute at least 24 hrs/week.

 - Critical thinking skills, expressiveness, and team-oriented attitude.

 - And you have to care about the work that you do!




NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience working with Excel spreadsheets.

 - Knowledge of ScanForm.

 - Familiarity with other HIV program areas beyond HIV testing.

 - General familiarity with other health programs in Côte d’Ivoire, beyond HIV.

 - Experience working with computers and digital tools in a professional setting.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Director
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.

We are looking for a marketing director to direct viral marketing campaigns for QED over the web, social media, and news outlets



BASIC REQUIREMENTS

We are looking for a ScanForm HIV Care and Treatment Field Support Officer in Malawi.

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.



We are looking for an infrastructure engineer to join our team Your responsibilities would include:

 - working with declarative tooling (NixOS + k3s + terraform) mostly on top of bare metal servers, with some cloud sprinkled in

 - planning infrastructure layout, selecting components, testing, and deploying them

 - designing reusable modules to ease deployment across many environments – our clients often expect their data to stay in their countries

 - improving dev tooling

 - maintaining monitoring and alerting

 - responding to downtimes (+ optional pager duty)

 - planning and building bare metal infrastructure for deployment in low to middle income countries

 - optional travel to learn more about problems we're trying to solve and set up hardware in local data centers



Requires a full-time commitment physically based in Warsaw, Poland, with a hybrid of remote and in-person work throughout the week. We expect at least 2-3 days per week in the office. The working hours are flexible, for example you do not need approval to take out a small chunk in the middle of the day to handle some personal business.



Desired skills include:

 - +4 years of experience

 - automation / declarative oriented mindset

 - broad knowledge of technology – including Linux internals, networking, databases, high level hardware performance, containers, performance tuning, security – we value genuine deep interest more than specific trivia knowledge, and you don't have to have experience with all of those areas

 - strong background in software engineering, version control, writing readable code and tests, robust design, and basic data structures and algorithms

 - working proficiency (≥C1) in speaking, reading, and typing English (≥45 words per minute)

 - logical reasoning and ability to express ideas clearly, both orally and written

 - willingness and interest in working with people from other cultures

 - emotional resilience and social intelligence

 - and you have to care about the work that you do

 - and you have to be optimistic at least sometimes



Below are additional skills that are a bonus, but are not required:

 - experience with NixOS, Kubernetes, Terraform

 - experience with bare metal, including hardware selection and on-site setup

 - ethical hacking experience

 - experience with programming and/or math competitions

 - willingness to go on an adventure

 - domain knowledge and/or interest in the sustainable development goals, particularly in public health, agriculture, and assisting developing countries



What can you expect:

 - fully declarative infrastructure, strong (but not absolute) avoidance of anything manual

 - no helm soup

 - unusual, socially conscious projects

 - low bureaucracy

 - significant ownership

 - ability and encouragement to explore technologies other than your main expertise

source_jobs.title: IT Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils. Our work happens at a national scale, and our funding comes from partners like the Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.

As we scale, we are looking for a highly trusted IT Operations Specialist to join our Warsaw team.

The Role

In this role, you’ll be responsible for the lifecycle of the hardware that powers our development and field operations across the globe. You will also be responsible for managing digital access internally, as well as externally. We are looking for a versatile professional with a background that spans both hardware and software administration.

Your core responsibilities will include:

 - Global procurement and logistics: Sourcing, configuring, and distributing laptops (Windows/Linux) and mobile devices for our staff in Poland and across our African offices.

 - Keeping track of the inventory: Knowing exactly where every piece of QED equipment is, what condition it’s in, and what happens the moment it breaks.

 - Security enforcement: Auditing and enforcing our personal computer security policy (FDE, handling of secrets).

 - Access Control: Handling onboarding, offboarding, and access to our internal platforms.

 - Android MDM: Managing our custom Mobile Device Management (MDM) service, built on the Android Management API. You will handle the procurement and logistics of devices used in the public health projects in the field. While this is an IT Operations role, the ability to read, write, and maintain the code of our custom MDM platform would be a big bonus.

Requirements:

 - High level of trust: You will have access to the most sensitive layers of our operation. Absolute integrity is non-negotiable.

 - Experience with IT administration: We require at least 4 years of industry experience. We expect a proactive, opinionated approach. You should have a clear vision and knowledge of modern tooling and the ability to establish robust conventions for access management and security.

 - Technical Proficiency: Proficiency in core technologies, including Git for version control, scripting and networking fundamentals.

 - Versatility: Comfort managing a fleet of Windows, Linux, and Android devices.

 - Language: Working proficiency (≥C1) in English.

Bonus: server infrastructure and willingness to travel

Our core medical services run on custom Kubernetes clusters hosted across a mix of VM providers and bare‑metal servers in Sub-Saharan Africa and beyond, and we are rapidly expanding to new countries. While not a core requirement, technical expertise to physically install, cable, and configure server racks on-site is a big plus. If you have an adventurous soul and a willingness to travel to our African offices to manage these hardware deployments firsthand, it would be an additional advantage (this added commitment would also come with higher pay).

What you can expect

 - Multisport

 - Flexible work hours (2-3 days in office)

 - High autonomy

 - Unusual socially conscious projects with genuine humanitarian purpose

 - Little bureaucracy

 - Significant ownership

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.


We are seeking a dedicated Executive Assistant (EA). The ideal candidate should be poised, presentable, and confident in expressing themselves. They should be well-organized, extroverted, detail-oriented, energetic, sharp, and enthusiastic about technology. Both men and women are encouraged to apply.

Typical tasks for the EA may include, in order of priority:

 - managing calendars, organizing meetings, arranging logistics and travel, writing professional e-mails and letters

 - writing and compiling clear reports of impact and progress across the company, and assisting executives with keeping track of projects and obligations with outside partners

 - assisting with direct communications and presentations, including public speaking and in-person meetings

 - organizing cloud-based digital assets in Google Workspace and similar platforms

 - assisting with spreadsheet-based financial management

 - have an eye for aesthetics and style


Our company executes international collaborations in science and technology, with experience working in 23 different developing countries. Due to the nature of this work, we have the following additional requirements:

 - Language:
   
   - Both oral and written fluency in English must be extremely high (e.g., CEFR C2), or native. Note that English speakers worldwide are welcome, and having a foreign accent can be attractive and well-received.
   
   - English touch typing speed should be at least 50 words per minute, and ideally closer to 80 words per minute. This capacity can be built up over time.
   
   - High fluency in at least one other foreign language, with special preferences for: French, Spanish, Hausa, Chichewa / Chinyanja, Swahili, Portuguese, and Chinese.

 - Logic and Technical Skills
   
   - Solid logic and reasoning skills
   
   - Basic number sense

 - Mission Alignment
   
   - Alignment with our mission and the pursuit of the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
   
   - Integrity, honesty, and an incorruptible moral code.

 - Ability and flexibility to travel:
   
   - For strategic reasons, when the EA is not on travel, it would be ideal for the EA to be based in one of the following countries: Malawi, South Africa, Philippines, Nigeria, Cameroon. If the candidate is not already based there, then he or she should be willing to move there.
   
   - Our executives are on heavy international travel, at least 40% of the time. The EA should be willing and able to travel alongside our executives across a wide portfolio of countries throughout the Global South, without hesitation or other conflicting commitments. The company will always ensure that the EA's accommodations are taken care of.
   
   - Candidates must possess the physical endurance and mental resilience required for extensive travel and sustained mobility in developing countries.

 - Exposure to STEM and/or the fine arts: Some domain expertise or prior experience in STEM, with preference to health, agriculture, and/or computer science, can be a plus. But this is not absolutely necessary and could be exchanged with other skills in the fine arts, with preference to writing, poetry, the visual arts, and vocal arts.

source_jobs.title: High School STEM Teacher – Philippines to Malawi
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai http://QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and artificial intelligence that empowers the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including epidemiological surveillance of HIV and malaria, nutrient analysis of crops and soils, and STEM education. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Global Fund, PEPFAR, and the Gates Foundation.

We are looking for an inspiring first-class High School STEM Teacher from the Philippines to join our team in Lilongwe, Malawi for at least one year, with the opportunity for extension if mutually agreeable. Key disciplines of interest are mathematics, mechatronics, physics, and chemistry. You would be riding on the coattails of an existing successful initiative, in which a new school has just been launched, and the first national-scale math olympiad in the history of Malawi has been executed by our organization and partners.

In addition to being fully funded, the job position also includes transportation to Malawi, and the provision of housing, board, and health insurance. Qualified applicants can earn between 90000 PHP and 200000 PHP or more per month depending on experience and ability, and can be negotiated.




MOTIVATION FOR THIS POSITION

STEM literacy is critical to the achievement and long-term sustainability of the Sustainable Development Goals. After 15 years of working on impact in over 23 developing countries, QED.ai http://QED.ai has observed that medical data, agricultural data, financial data, and business data are all overwhelmingly underused. Few public policies are informed by data or scientific thinking. Data is only used as a means of reporting particular indicators to upstream donors for reasons that few understand or remember. Scarcity of STEM capacity prevents local actors from engaging critically with data, questioning assumptions, making non-trivial computations, and being entrusted to manage large programs without continued reliance on international NGOs.

Mathematics and Science must be the foundation of decision making in public health and agronomy. Technology and Engineering must supply practical techniques for applying science and math to the real-world.  We aim to prioritize the indoctrination of core STEM skills to the next generation of bright people who will become tomorrow’s leaders.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Teach STEM each day to precocious children that are interested in learning more.  Our primary focus is mathematics. Secondary focus is mechatronics if the expertise is available. You do not need to be proficient at both.

 - Start with private education with selected students, focused on creative problem solving. Expand as the curriculum is stabilized and adapted to local needs.

 - Co-develop high school lesson plans and curriculums, focusing on practical STEM topics that QED.ai http://QED.ai has identified as essential for the next generation to know.

 - Lead a weekly math circle open to a small group of community members, including both adults and children, to solve creative and fun math problems at the children’s level.

 - Planning and adminstration of national-scale STEM contests, such as Mathematical Kangaroo https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematical_Kangaroo and the Pan-African Mathematical Olympiad https://www.pamoofficial.org. You will have assistance in administration from local staff, but must also be willing to engage in some administration work as necessary for the greater good.

 - Work with QED on governmental advocacy to set higher standards for STEM proficiency.

 - Assist executives with business development in education, and building stronger partnerships in STEM collaboration between Asia and Africa.




REQUIREMENTS




TECHNICAL

 - Proficiency in teaching core STEM subjects at the junior high and high school level. Candidates should be proficient in Mathematics, or Physic

source_jobs.title: Assistant(e) d'impression - Côte d'Ivoire
source_jobs.description:
CONTEXTE

Dans le cadre des efforts continus visant à renforcer les systèmes de données sanitaires en Côte d'Ivoire, QED, en collaboration avec des partenaires locaux, recherche un(e) assistant(e) d'impression ScanForm à temps partiel pour aider à la préparation et à la production des registres de santé papier utilisés dans les programmes soutenus par ScanForm.

QED est le développeur de ScanForm, une plateforme de saisie et de traitement de données numériques conçue pour transformer les registres de santé papier en données structurées et analysables. ScanForm permet aux agents de santé de première ligne de continuer à utiliser des outils papier familiers tout en permettant la numérisation, la validation et l'intégration rapides des données dans les systèmes d'information en aval. La plateforme met l'accent sur la qualité des données, la facilité d'utilisation et l'alignement avec les systèmes nationaux de reporting.

En Côte d'Ivoire, ScanForm s'appuie sur des registres imprimés de haute qualité pour garantir la précision de la saisie des données dans les établissements de santé. L'assistant d'impression jouera un rôle essentiel dans le soutien de ce travail en préparant, imprimant, organisant et contrôlant la qualité des documents papier utilisés sur le terrain.




OBJECTIF DU POSTE

L'objectif de ce poste est de soutenir la mise en œuvre de ScanForm en veillant à ce que les registres imprimés et les documents papier soient produits avec précision, cohérence et conformément aux spécifications, en accordant une attention particulière aux détails et au contrôle de la qualité.




ÉTENDUE DES TÂCHES ET PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITÉS

L'assistant d'impression ScanForm sera chargé des activités suivantes :

 - Préparer et imprimer les registres ScanForm et les documents papier connexes conformément aux modèles et spécifications établis.

 - Utiliser les imprimantes et le matériel informatique de base utilisés pour la préparation et l'impression des documents.

 - Effectuer des contrôles de qualité de routine sur les documents imprimés, en s'assurant que le formatage, la pagination, l'alignement et l'exhaustivité sont corrects.

 - Organiser, étiqueter et emballer les registres imprimés pour la distribution ou le stockage.

 - Maintenir un espace de travail propre et organisé.

 - Signaler en temps utile les erreurs d'impression, les problèmes d'équipement ou les besoins en fournitures au personnel de supervision.

 - Assurer le suivi de base des documents imprimés (par exemple, quantités produites, dates, versions), selon les besoins.

 - Suivre scrupuleusement et systématiquement les procédures établies.




QUALIFICATIONS ET PROFIL REQUIS

Formation

 - Bac + 2 en logistique.

 - La capacité à lire et à écrire clairement en français est requise.

Expérience

 - Une expérience préalable dans l'utilisation d'une imprimante, d'un photocopieur ou d'un équipement informatique de base est fortement appréciée.

 - Une expérience dans des rôles exigeant une grande attention aux détails (par exemple, soutien administratif, entretien ménager, gestion des stocks, impression ou travail similaire) est un atout.

 - Une expérience préalable dans des programmes de santé n'est pas requise.

Compétences techniques et professionnelles

 - Très grande attention aux détails et capacité à suivre les instructions avec précision.

 - Connaissances informatiques de base (ouverture de fichiers, sélection des paramètres d'impression, enregistrement de documents).

 - Capacité à apprendre et à appliquer de manière cohérente des procédures standardisées.

 - Aisance dans l'exécution de tâches répétitives avec un haut niveau de précision.

Langues

 - Maîtrise du français (parlé et écrit) requise.

 - La connaissance des langues locales est un atout.

Qualités personnelles

 - Fiable, ponctuel et organisé.

 - Patience et minutie dans l'exécution des tâches.

 - Fierté de produire un travail de haute qualité.

 - Capacité à travailler de manière autonome tou

source_jobs.title: Data Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV/malaria/TB, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, and the CDC.

We are looking for a mid/senior Data Platform Engineer (backend) to join our team in Warsaw. Desired skills include:

 - experience designing and maintaining data pipelines using ETL and/or ELT approaches, with the ability to reason about trade-offs rather than defaulting to a single pattern

 - understanding of data pipeline reliability concepts, including idempotency, backfills, and handling late or corrected data

 - ability to structure data systems into clear layers (e.g. raw, cleaned, curated) and reason about their different purposes and guarantees

 - experience deciding between batch, micro-batch, and streaming approaches based on latency, correctness, and operational complexity

 - strong background in software engineering, version control, writing readable code and tests, robust design, and basic data structures and algorithms

 - ability to conceive logical software architectures and express yourself clearly, both verbally and in writing

 - willingness to tackle a wide array of problems and technologies

 - willingness to participate in regular design sessions, code reviews, and working in teams

 - development primarily on UNIX-based or OSX-Darwin platforms

 - working proficiency (≥C1) in speaking, reading, and typing English (≥45 words per minute)

 - willingness and interest in working with people from other cultures

 - emotional resilience and social intelligence

 - and you have to care about the work that you do

 - and you have to be optimistic at least sometimes, although pessimism tends to be useful

Below are additional skills that are a bonus, but are not required:

 - understanding of analytical data modeling concepts, including how to transform raw data into analytics-ready datasets and define clear data semantics for downstream consumers

 - experience with Python, Django, DBT, Dagster, Luigi or Clickhouse

 - experience with containerization (Docker), Terraform, Kubernetes or Nix

 - knowledge and experience with data warehousing, online analytical processing, metadata management, dimensional modeling, and relational database theory

 - experience with programming and/or math competitions

 - product-oriented mindset

 - willingness to go on an adventure

 - domain knowledge and/or interest in the sustainable development goals, particularly in public health, agriculture, and assisting developing countries

What can you expect:

 - unusual, socially conscious projects

 - significant ownership

 - a mix of product, internal platform, and client integration work

 - ability and encouragement to explore technologies other than your main expertise

 - optional travel to learn more about problems we're trying to solve

Requires a full-time commitment physically based in Warsaw, Poland, with a hybrid of remote and in-person work throughout the week. We expect at least 2-3 days per week in the office. The working hours are flexible, for example you do not need approval to take out a small chunk in the middle of the day to handle some personal business.

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer -  Côte d'Ivoire
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai http://QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and artificial intelligence that empowers the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, such as epidemiological surveillance and control of HIV, TB, and malaria. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Global Fund, CDC, and the Gates Foundation.

We are looking for a data engineer to assist with both (a) the construction of medical data processing pipelines and (b) the analysis of national-scale healthcare data. Combine your reasoning skills with empathy for others to bring voice to the untold masses struggling to receive adequate health services across the Global South. Use numbers, technology, and visualizations to build data-driven stories that will change the world.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Interest in public health and using data to save human lives.

 - Strong logical reasoning and critical thinking skills, puzzle-solving skills, and attention to detail.

 - Experience with computer programming.

 - Fluency working with Excel spreadsheets / Google sheets, and analyzing numbers.

 - Proficiency in speaking, reading, and composing clear and correct English.

 - Typing speed of at least 35 words per minute.

 - Enthusiasm for information technology.

 - An eye for clarity in the visual presentation of data.

 - Full-time employment of at least 40 hours per week.

 - Good fluency in English speaking and writing.

 - Expressiveness, emotional intelligence, and team-oriented attitude.

 - And you have to care about the work that you do!


NICE TO HAVES

 - Domain expertise, experience, or energetic interest in medicine and human health.

 - Experience working with databases and writing SQL queries.

 - Some experience with basic statistics, or monitoring and evaluation.

source_jobs.title: Forward-Deployed Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils, and surveillance of HIV and malaria at national-scale in multiple African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Gates Foundation, U.S. government, WHO, the EU, the Global Fund, and more.


We are looking for a Forward-Deployed Data Analyst to work on health data systems and travel frequently to African countries such as Malawi, Cameroon, Cote d’Ivoire, Nigeria, Mozambique, and various other countries throughout the Global South.




BACKGROUND

Information is essential for host nations to manage their health systems effectively. and even more so in light of shifts in foreign aid funding. With complete, accurate, and near real-time data, health programs can continually focus efforts where most needed and improve their impact. Capturing high quality data affordably and at national scale has been an insurmountable task, until now. ScanForm, developed by private sector company QED.ai http://QED.ai, uses artificial intelligence to instantly digitize data from hand-written information on paper using an inexpensive handheld smartphone. It then instantly integrates that data with canonical health data systems such as DHIS2, OpenMRS, and others, and automates all analysis and reporting functions. The end result is fully digitized and individual data acquired at far less cost than traditional all-electronic approaches.

QED.ai http://QED.ai is looking for a Forward-Deployed Data Analyst that welcomes the challenge of working with governments in Africa to lead high-impact change through ScanForm’s leapfrog technology. You will work directly with ministerial officers and international organizations on the construction of new digital health data pipelines based on ScanForm, starting with preliminary scoping and form design, and ending with full deployment at national scale. Logical reasoning, language fluency, emotional intelligence, clarity of communication, and cultural sensitivity are key.

You’ll contribute to building well-structured, intuitive tools for capturing data efficiently. This includes standardizing identifiers for specimens, patients, diseases, diagnoses, and treatments. You will help ensure that data is captured on time, is automatically vetted for quality using logical rules of mathematics and public health, and that data is converted into dashboards that tell clear stories to identify actionable trends. You’ll work closely with end users to understand their needs and help them extract relevant information, including high-level stakeholders at the Ministry of Health and healthcare providers working in clinics and the community, always with the aim of achieving a large, meaningful impact that matters. You will also communicate, sharing stories of how ScanForm makes a difference for governments and their constituents.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Engage with Ministries of Health in Africa, as well as donor organizations such as the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), U.S. Department of State, UNICEF, to fully understand all current data processing workflows and pains, and governmental requirements for data capture and data analysis.

 - Build data capture and data management solutions for our clients, using training that QED provides. This design includes crafting of questionnaires, layout of data elements, visual aesthetics, and identifiers, while also being sensitive to client needs.

 - Help local and international agencies understand the many benefits of converting health systems to ScanForm.

 - Develop indicator dashboards analyses that support data use and public health decision-making, using peer-reviewed SQL and training that QED provides.

 - Communicate regularly, effectively, and in a timely manner with

source_jobs.title: ScanForm Country Lead
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils, and surveillance of HIV, TB, malaria, and other epidemics at national-scale in multiple African countries. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Gates Foundation, U.S. government, the WHO, the EU, the Global Fund, and many more.

We are looking for a Country Lead based in selected countries in Africa. These may include: Nigeria, Mozambique, Malawi, Cameroon, and other countries, depending on where projects go to scale. The Country Lead should be eager for the challenge of leading high-impact change through ScanForm’s leapfrog technology. You will serve as the frontline in-country interface for QED with ministerial officers and international organizations, managing their expectations, and lead international teams in the planning and execution of workplans to design and deploy ScanForm at national scale. Language fluency, diplomacy, emotional intelligence, ability to lead and collaborate, and cultural sensitivity are key. You’ll contribute to building well-structured, intuitive tools for capturing data efficiently; standardizing identifiers for specimens, patients, cards, pages, books, diseases, diagnoses, and treatments; and ensuring that data is captured on time, adheres to mathematical and clinical sensibilities, and is converted into dashboards that tell clear stories to quickly identify actionable trends and patterns. You’ll work closely with end users to understand their needs and help them extract relevant information, including high-level stakeholders at the Ministry of Health and healthcare providers working in clinics and the community, always with the aim of achieving a large, meaningful impact that matters. You will also communicate, sharing the stories of how ScanForm is making a difference in the country you work in.




SCOPE OF WORK

 - Engage with Ministries of Health and Agriculture in Africa, as well as donor organizations such as the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), U.S. Department of State, Global Fund, UNICEF, etc., to first fully understand all current data processing workflows and pains, and governmental requirements for data capture and data analysis.

 - Help local and international agencies understand the many financial and epidemiological benefits of converting health systems to ScanForm, including streamlining of business processes, and achieving much more monitoring and evaluation with much less. Express cost-benefit arguments and analyses convincingly and clearly.

 - Manage and build local teams in target countries. Listen to their needs, get them to open up and share their thoughts, and be an empathetic and receptive ear for staff that seek your help.

 - Lead collaboration with other in-country Implementing Partners, as appropriate.

 - Communicate regularly, effectively, and in a timely manner with in-country funders, the host nation government, and QED’s central staff and offices, particularly when there are challenges on the ground that may require intervention from headquarters.

 - Lead discussions and seminars about ScanForm and health system strengthening.

 - Assist with the co-design of data capture and data management solutions for our clients. This design should incorporate crafting of questionnaires, layout of data elements, visual aesthetics, and identifiers, while also being sensitive to our client’s needs.

 - Contribute to and supervise the development of indicator dashboards, queries, and analyses that support data use and public health decision-making.

 - Assist with representing and presenting ScanForm at conferences and technical working groups.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Working proficiency (ILR 4/5, or CEFR ≥C1) in speaking, reading, writing, and typing in English.

 - For F

source_jobs.title: Innovation Funding Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Many challenges in food security are road-blocked by the scarcity of sufficient environmental data for making decisions. Most agricultural players have no means of rapidly assessing the health of their soils, plants, seeds, and fertilizers, particularly in rural areas.

Consequently, QED is developing novel hardware sensors for collecting environmental data more affordably and easily, using techniques such as infrared spectroscopy and automated chemistry. Our work lies at the intersection of mechatronics, chemistry, physics, and computing.

QED is looking to hire Innovation Funding Specialists with a solid background in the natural sciences (e.g., physics, chemistry, agronomy) to assist with grant proposal writing, pitch deck preparation, and communication with funding bodies and partners.

Key responsibilities:

 - Monitor and identify national and international funding opportunities (EU, global agencies, foundations, etc.).

 - Write and submit high-quality grant proposals, including technical and non-technical content.

 - Conduct desk research on relevant scientific and funding topics.

 - Collaborate with internal teams (technical, R&D, business) to gather input and align funding strategies with company goals.

 - Prepare promotional and communication materials (pitch decks, articles, whitepapers, project summaries, one-pagers).

 - Maintain a database of grant opportunities, submission deadlines, and reporting requirements.

 - Maintain communication with partners, funding agencies, and collaborators as needed.

 - Support scientific outreach and visibility of the company in relevant scientific and innovation ecosystems.



Requirements:

 - Excellent writing skills in English, especially in scientific and technical communication.

 - Critical thinking, write logically.

 - Proven experience in grant writing, research communication, or fundraising at least 3 years.

 - Be ahead of time with proposal submissions.

 - Communicate easily with people from different backgrounds and timezones.

 - Strong analytical and online research skills.

 - Ability to translate complex scientific ideas into clear, persuasive narratives.

 - Organized, proactive, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.

 - Familiarity with Horizon Europe, EIC, Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, USAID, or similar funding schemes is a plus.

 - English fluency: B2 or higher (hard requirement)

 - English typing speed >= 40 words per minute



Nice to have:

 - Degree (min. MSc) in a relevant scientific field: agronomy, biology, chemistry, physics, environmental science, or similar.



Compensation is competitive and commensurate to experience.



Work with nice people!

See more of the world!



Online application form: https://about.qed.ai/careers

For special inquiries, e-mail: careers@qed.ai jobs@qed.ai

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist – Warsaw
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils and foods, and surveillance of HIV and malaria at national-scale in multiple African countries. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations, such as the Gates Foundation, the Polish Ministry of Agriculture, EU funding programmes, the Global Fund, and others.



We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team in Warsaw, Poland working on ScanSpectrum (https://scanspectrum.qed.ai), a low-cost and handheld spectrophotometry kit being used to assess quality parameters of food and agricultural products.



SCOPE OF WORK

 - Build and improve regression models for spectroscopic data (e.g., PLSR, SVR, Extra Trees)

 - Plan sampling strategies and real-world experimental design with a strong statistical mindset (representativeness, power, coverage across varieties/regions/seasons/batches).

 - Design robust training/evaluation pipelines: preprocessing, feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, and proper cross-validation.

 - Apply chemometrics best practices to spectral data (baseline/scatter effects, derivatives, outliers, drift).

 - Diagnose model failures and data issues (instrument effects, label noise, dataset shift) and propose fixes.

 - Construct dashboards/reports to present and visualize data analytics.

 - Collaborate with engineering/product to package and deploy models (model export, versioning, monitoring metrics).

 - Assist in effective communication with scientific institutes, companies, or researchers that QED currently collaborates with or wishes to collaborate with.

 - Collaborate with governmental, medical, agronomic, and computer science teams in the composition of research papers and impact reports.
   


REQUIREMENTS



TECHNICAL

 - Proficiency with core ideas in statistics.

 - Formal academic studies in statistics, data science, or software engineering, coupled with some practical experience with analyzing real-world datasets.

 - Ability to use computer programming to wrangle, inspect, and analyze statistical data.

 - Proficiency with git and Python-based programming environments, preferably with CI/CD.

 - Practical proficiency in analyzing substantial real-world datasets with
   
   - traditional statistical techniques, such as regression, hypothesis testing, survey design, time series, and RCTs, and also
   
   - with modern machine learning methods, such as decision trees, boosting and neural networks.

 - Tenacity and curiosity to acquire domain expertise as needed, enabling rigorous contextualization of statistical challenges within established scientific frameworks (e.g., biology, chemistry, agronomy).


GENERAL

 - Working proficiency (≥C1) in speaking and reading English, and capable of typing English with a speed of at least ≥45 words per minute.

 - Logical reasoning and ability to express oneself clearly, both orally and in writing.

 - Willingness and interest in working with people from other cultures. Emotional resilience and social intelligence to communicate and work with collaborators from around the world, including Europe, Africa, Asia, and the USA.

 - Don’t be afraid of getting your hands dirty.

 - … and you have to care about the work that you do!



Below are additional skills that are a bonus, but are not required:



 - Prior experience with spectroscopy (Vis/NIR/MIR) and chemometrics in practice.

 - Understanding of spectroscopy physics (e.g., overtones/combination bands, scattering, path length effects).

 - Experience with calibration transfer, drift monitoring, or multi-instrument datasets.

 - Domain familiarity: food chemistry, agriculture, grain quality, soil/plant analysis, lab reference methods.

 - Domain knowledge and/or interest in the sustainable development goals, particularly

source_jobs.title: Lead Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.



We are looking for the Lead Infrastructure Engineer to shape and evolve the technical foundation that powers our systems. This is a hands-on leadership role where you will define architectural direction, elevate engineering standards, and stay deeply involved in building and operating infrastructure. As a team lead, you will guide critical decisions, mentor engineers, and drive a long-term vision for reliable systems operating across multiple countries.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - owning and evolving the infrastructure architecture, tooling strategy, and long-term technical decisions

 - working with declarative tooling (NixOS + k3s + terraform) mostly on top of bare metal servers, with some cloud sprinkled in

 - planning infrastructure layout, selecting components, testing, and deploying them

 - planning and leading the building of bare metal infrastructure for deployment in low to middle income countries

 - handling communication (both internal and external)

 - participating in hiring, evaluate and interview candidates

 - optional traveling to learn more about problems we're trying to solve and set up hardware in local data centers
   
   

The role requires a full-time commitment physically based in Warsaw, Poland, with a hybrid of remote and in-person work throughout the week. We expect at least 2-3 days per week in the office. The working hours are flexible, for example you do not need approval to take out a small chunk in the middle of the day to handle some personal business.



Desired skills include:

 - 6+ years of infrastructure or systems engineering experience, with at least 1–2 years in a lead role

 - demonstrated ability to scope, plan, and delegate technical work and to hold yourself and others accountable for outcomes

 - broad knowledge of technology: Linux internals, networking, databases, containers, hardware performance, security; we value genuine depth and curiosity over checklist coverage

 - strong software engineering fundamentals: version control, readable code, tests, robust design, basic data structures and algorithms

 - working proficiency in English (≥ C1 spoken and written; ≥ 45 wpm typed)

 - clear thinker who can express ideas well, both in writing and in conversation

 - social intelligence and emotional resilience; you will work across cultures, time zones, and high-stakes situations



Below are additional skills that are a bonus, but are not required:

 - experience with NixOS, Kubernetes, Terraform

 - experience with bare metal, including hardware selection and on-site setup

 - ethical hacking experience

 - experience with programming and/or math competitions

 - willingness to go on an adventure

 - domain knowledge and/or interest in the sustainable development goals, particularly in public health, agriculture, and assisting developing countries



What can you expect:

 - significant ownership, direct influence on team structure, standards, and hiring

 - fully declarative infrastructure, strong (but not absolute) avoidance of anything manual

 - no helm soup

 - unusual, socially conscious projects

 - low bureaucracy

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst – Lilongwe
source_jobs.description:
QED.ai http://QED.ai (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and artificial intelligence that empowers the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including epidemiological surveillance of HIV and malaria, and in-situ nutrient analysis of crops and soils using spectroscopy and computer vision. Our funding comes from philanthropic and governmental organizations such as the Global Fund, CDC, USAID, and the Gates Foundation.

We are looking for a medical data analyst to assist with both (a) the construction of medical data processing pipelines and (b) the analysis of national-scale healthcare data. Combine your mathematical talent with empathy for others to bring voice to the untold masses struggling to receive adequate health services across the Global South. Use numbers, technology, and visualizations to build data-driven stories that will change the world.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Incessant passion for public health and using data to save human life.

 - Strong logical reasoning skills, puzzle-solving skills, and attention to detail.

 - Fluency working with Excel spreadsheets / Google sheets, and analyzing numbers.

 - Proficiency in speaking, reading, and composing clear and correct English.

 - English typing speed of at least 35 words per minute.

 - Enthusiasm for information technology.

 - An eye for clarity in the visual presentation of data.

 - Highly preferred full-time employment of at least 40 hours per week. Promising students with exceptional skills should contribute at least 24 hrs/week.

 - Good fluency in English speaking and writing (typing > 40 wpm).

 - Critical thinking skills, expressiveness, and team-oriented attitude.

 - And you have to care about the work that you do!


NICE TO HAVES

 - Domain expertise, experience, or energetic interest in medicine and human health.

 - Experience working with databases and writing SQL queries.

 - Some experience with monitoring and evaluation, or basic computer programming, or basic statistics.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Specialist
source_jobs.description:
QED (https://qed.ai) is a tech company focused on public health and food security in Sub-Saharan Africa. We build the digital infrastructure and AI used at the intersection of aid and scientific inquiry, including surveillance of HIV and malaria, and nutrient analysis of crops and soils, working at national-scale in several African countries. Our funding comes from multiple philanthropic and governmental organizations, including the Global Fund, Gates Foundation, CDC, and USAID.
